identity.name: Malicious VS Code Extension Reads Credentials On Activation (T1176 / T1195)
identity.summary: A developer installs 'helpful-ai-assistant' — an unverified-publisher VS Code extension whose package.json declares activationEvents:['*'] (loads on every workspace). Its dist/extension.js activate() reads ${HOME}/.aws/credentials and ${HOME}/.ssh/id_rsa and beacons them to C2 (gated on CORTEXSIM_C2_URL). Cortex Cloud Code flags the wildcard activation + credential reads at scan time; XDR catch…
identity.description: IDE extensions run with the developer's full privileges and load silently, which makes them an ideal persistence + credential-access foothold on an agentic developer endpoint. The helpful-ai-assistant extension in cortex-malicious-agentic-pack ships package.json with activationEvents:['*'] (it activates on every workspace, not on a specific command), an unknown-publisher field, and an extension.js whose activate() builds paths to ${HOME}/.aws/credentials and ${HOME}/.ssh/id_rsa, fs.readFileSync's them, and POSTs the payload to a C2 host (gated on CORTEXSIM_C2_URL so static scanning is safe). Detection is layered: (1) Cortex Cloud Code static scan of package.json + extension.js flags wildcard activation, the credential-file reads, and the outbound beacon; (2) the agentic_egress EAL plugin drives a real .vsix fetch over HTTPS, which NGFW flags as a non-canonical Marketplace CDN and a marketplace risk score flags the unverified/new/overbroad-permission publisher; (3) at activation, XDR catches the VS Code child process reading credential files and opening an outbound connection to a non-Microsoft domain. A correlation rule fuses these into one incident.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1176 — Malicious IDE extension with wildcard activation + credential read (CortexSim cortex-malicious-agentic-pack/vscode/helpful-ai-assistant)

prerequisites.description: Cortex XDR agent installed and reporting healthy on the developer endpoint.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i traps

prerequisites.description: cortex-malicious-agentic-pack content present and cortexsim-eal-cli available (scenario SIM-KOI-004 requirements).

payload.code:
# step-1: static scan of the extension source (safe — read only).
EXT_ROOT=sources/cortex-malicious-agentic-pack/vscode/helpful-ai-assistant
ls -la "$EXT_ROOT"/extension/
jq '.contributes, .activationEvents, .extensionDependencies' "$EXT_ROOT"/extension/package.json
grep -nE 'fetch|http|XMLHttpRequest|webRequest|\.aws/credentials|\.ssh/' "$EXT_ROOT"/extension/dist/*.js | head -20 || true
# step-2: fetch the .vsix via the agentic_egress EAL plugin (canary CDN only).
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/koi-004.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: VS Code child process reads ~/.aws/credentials and ~/.ssh/id_rsa during activate().
Outbound beacon from the VS Code extension host to a non-Microsoft / non-canonical CDN.

iocs.context: Non-canonical Marketplace CDN / beacon C2 host for the malicious extension in the lab.

biocs.name: KOI-004 VSCode Extension Reads AWS Credentials And SSH Keys In Activate
biocs.description: Code scan — extension reads ${HOME}/.aws/credentials and ${HOME}/.ssh/ via fs.readFile in activate(). A VS Code / node child process reads credential and SSH key files during extension activation.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter event_sub_type in (ENUM.FILE_OPEN, ENUM.FILE_READ)
| filter action_file_path contains_any (".aws/credentials", ".ssh/id_rsa")
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("code", "node", "Code", "code-server") or causality_actor_process_image_name in ("code", "node", "Code")

biocs.name: KOI-004 VSCode Extension Activate Beacons Outbound To Non Microsoft Domain
biocs.description: Code scan — extension activate() opens outbound HTTPS to non-Microsoft domain. The VS Code extension host process opens an outbound connection to a non-Microsoft / non-canonical domain right after activation.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("code", "node", "Code", "code-server") or causality_actor_process_image_name in ("code", "node", "Code")
| filter dst_action_external_hostname not contains "microsoft.com"
| filter dst_action_external_hostname not contains "visualstudio.com"
| filter dst_action_external_hostname not contains "vscode.dev"
| filter action_external_port in (80, 443)

xql_queries.name: KOI-004 VSCode Extension Manifest Declares Wildcard Activation Events
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("jq", "grep", "cat", "cortexcloud", "checkov")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "package.json"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("activationEvents", "helpful-ai-assistant")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: KOI-004 VSCode Marketplace VSIX Fetch From Non Canonical CDN
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| filter dst_action_external_hostname contains "cortexsim-canary" or dns_query_name contains "cortexsim-canary"
| filter action_external_url contains "/vsx/" or action_external_url contains ".vsix"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, dst_action_external_hostname, action_external_url

xql_queries.name: KOI-004 Extension Marketplace Risk Unverified Publisher Overbroad Permissions
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "helpful-ai-assistant"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("activationEvents", "package.json", "unknown-publisher")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: KOI-004 VSCode Child Process Emits Post Install Beacon Traffic
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| filter causality_actor_process_image_name in ("code", "node", "Code", "code-server") or actor_process_image_name in ("code", "node")
| filter dst_action_external_hostname contains "cortexsim-canary" or dst_action_external_hostname not contains "microsoft.com"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, dst_action_external_hostname, action_external_port

correlation_rules.name: Agentic Supply Chain — Malicious IDE Extension Credential Theft
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(KOI-004 VSCode Extension Reads AWS Credentials And SSH Keys In Activate) OR BIOC(KOI-004 VSCode Extension Activate Beacons Outbound To Non Microsoft Domain)

analytics_modules: Cortex Cloud Code — IDE-extension manifest + source scanner (wildcard activation, credential reads, beacon)
Cortex XDR Analytics — editor child process reading credential files then beaconing outbound

products.evidence_query: Cortex Cloud Code finding: wildcard activationEvents + ~/.aws/~/.ssh read + outbound beacon in sources/cortex-malicious-agentic-pack/vscode/helpful-ai-assistant

preset = xdr_data | filter action_file_path contains_any (".aws/credentials", ".ssh/id_rsa") and causality_actor_process_image_name in ("code","node") | fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name
Correlation CR-KOI-0004 fuses the credential-read and outbound-beacon BIOCs into one IDE-extension credential-theft incident.

use_cases.name: Developer-Tool Extension Risk
use_cases.description: Detect a malicious VS Code extension with wildcard activation that reads developer credentials and beacons out, across static (Cortex Cloud Code) and runtime (XDR/NGFW) telemetry.

test_cases.objective: Cortex Cloud Code flags wildcard activation + credential reads.
success_criteria: XQL `KOI-004 VSCode Extension Manifest Declares Wildcard Activation Events` returns >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Runtime credential read + beacon stitch into one incident.
success_criteria: BIOC `KOI-004 VSCode Extension Reads AWS Credentials And SSH Keys In Activate` fires.

preventive_controls: Allowlist IDE extensions to verified publishers; block extensions with activationEvents:['*'] from unverified publishers.
Run Cortex Cloud Code scanning of extension manifests + bundled JS as an install gate.
Store developer cloud credentials in short-lived SSO/STS rather than ~/.aws/credentials on disk.
Restrict developer-endpoint egress to an allowlist that excludes arbitrary beacon destinations.
detection_engineering: Alert when an editor child process (code/node) reads ~/.aws/credentials or ~/.ssh/id_rsa.
Flag IDE-extension manifests with wildcard activation + unverified publisher + network/auth permissions.
Correlate the credential read with an immediate outbound beacon to a non-Microsoft domain into one incident.

references.title: Unit 42 — VS Code Marketplace and IDE-Extension Risk
